name: Allegheny Ludlum Brackenridge
description_short: Allegheny College is a private liberal arts institution in Pennsylvania offering undergraduate education with merit scholarships, not a consumer finance company.
description_long: Allegheny College is a private liberal arts college located in Meadville, Pennsylvania, founded to provide undergraduate education across diverse academic disciplines. The institution is not a consumer finance company and does not offer payday loans, credit alternatives, personal loans, or any financial services to consumers. Instead, Allegheny College operates as an educational institution serving high school seniors and undergraduate students seeking a four-year degree program. The college offers financial aid packages, merit-based scholarships (including Visit Grants up to $4,000), and need-based assistance to admitted students, with 90% of students receiving some form of financial assistance. What distinguishes Allegheny is its unusual-combinations model, requiring students to pursue both a major and minor in different academic areas, combined with guaranteed research and capstone experiences. The college ranks highly in U.S. News & World Report categories including Most Innovative Schools (#13), Senior Capstone (#12), and Best Undergraduate Teaching (#36). Post-graduation outcomes show 98% employment or enrollment in graduate/professional programs within six months, with 57% entering careers and 36% pursuing advanced degrees. The college serves approximately 43 states and 52 countries in its student body and is Pennsylvania's first carbon-neutral college. This is fundamentally an educational institution, not a consumer finance provider, and was incorrectly categorized in the payday-alternatives segment.
services: Undergraduate degree programs across liberal arts disciplines
Merit-based scholarships including Visit Grants ($1,000-$4,000 annually)
Graduate pathway programs (including 4+1 education degree with Mercyhurst University)
pros: 98% of graduates employed or in graduate/professional programs within 6 months of graduation
90% of students receive some form of financial assistance, demonstrating robust aid availability
Unique major-minor combination requirement develops versatile career skills valued by employers
Guaranteed research and hands-on learning opportunities embedded in curriculum
Strong rankings in innovative teaching (#36), social mobility (#19), and undergraduate research (#32)
Visit Grant program ($1,000/year renewable) provides direct scholarship incentive
Pennsylvania's first carbon-neutral college showing institutional sustainability commitment
cons: This is an educational institution, not a consumer finance company—fundamentally miscategorized
No financial services, loans, or credit products offered to the general public
College costs and aid packages not detailed on homepage; full financial information requires separate inquiry
Limited to serving high school seniors and admitted undergraduate students, not general consumers
No emergency funding, short-term credit, or alternative lending products available
best_for: High school seniors seeking liberal arts education with merit scholarship opportunities
Students interested in interdisciplinary study combining major and minor fields
Prospective undergraduates seeking strong post-graduation employment outcomes
diagnosis: Allegheny College is a private liberal arts educational institution, not a consumer finance company, and should not appear in any finance category. This profile exists only to correct a categorical error in CreditDoc's database. The college serves admitted undergraduate students seeking degree programs and financial aid, not consumers seeking loans or credit products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
